free_text: Combined study of the mechanism of the reactions
Mo-95,98(p,n)Tc-95,98 and the nuclear level density
The neutron detector was a stilbene crystal
of diameter 63 mm and height 39 mm with an FEU-30
Cross sections were determined as a function of
proton energy for two intervals of neutron energy
(from 0 to Ep + Q and from 1.0 to Ep + Q MeV).
Since the threshold of the detector is 0.6 MeV, the
integral spectra are given with a neutron energy 1 MeV.
The cross sections in the interval from 0 to 1 MeV
were found by extrapolation of the energy spectra by
sig(En) = C x E(n) x exp(-E(n)/T).
Values of T and C were determined on the basis of the
portion of the experimental spectrum in the region
From compiler: E-MIN/MAX were calculated from the
information given about intervals of neutron energy
SD: Two REACTION codes were combined in
SD: The table was reorganized so that EN
becomes the innermost independent variable. Values were
added to the col. E-MAX where it was missed. ANALYSIS
